_description: Widescreen insight card for presentations, YouTube thumbnails, and desktop decks. Two-column layout: bold heading on the left, supporting body text on the right, separated by a vertical accent divider. Supports an optional full-bleed background image with a dark overlay. Keep bg and ink consistent across all slides; vary only accent.

_slots.heading: REQUIRED — the main insight. 2–10 words. Displayed at 88px bold serif in the left column.
_slots.body: optional — supporting explanation. 1–3 sentences. Displayed in the right column at 30px. When omitted, the heading expands to fill the full width and the vertical divider is hidden.
_slots.eyebrow: optional — short uppercase label above the rule (e.g. 'LESSON', 'INSIGHT', 'TIP'). Displayed in accent color.
_slots.footer: optional — bottom-left text. Attribution, handle, or brand name (e.g. '@yourname').
_slots.image: optional — background image. Local path relative to data JSON or a URL. A dark overlay (tinted with bg color at 74% opacity) is applied automatically so text remains legible. Works best with moody, dark, or desaturated photos. When omitted, the slide uses the solid bg color.
_slots.bg: optional — background hex color. Also used as the overlay tint when an image is present. Use the SAME value on every slide in a deck. Default: #0c0b09
_slots.ink: optional — primary text hex color. Use the SAME value on every slide in a deck. Default: #eae6de
_slots.accent: optional — accent hex color for eyebrow, rule, and vertical divider. VARY per slide. Default: #d4a054

title: Lessons from Building in Public

slides.heading: Start before you're ready
slides.body: Waiting for the perfect moment is just procrastination wearing a mask. Ship the ugly first version — you'll learn more in one week of real feedback than a month of planning.

slides.heading: Share the process, not just the outcome
slides.body: People connect with struggle and iteration. The messy middle is the interesting part — polished results are forgettable.

slides.heading: Consistency compounds
slides.body: One post won't change anything. Three hundred posts will change everything. Show up daily and let time do the heavy lifting.

slides.heading: Your audience is smaller than you think — and that's fine
